%%% Helper function to check if a character is a vowel
is_vowel(Char) ->
    Char == 'a' ;
    Char == 'e' ;
    Char == 'i' ;
    Char == 'o' ;
    Char == 'u'.

%%% Helper function to calculate frequency and first appearance index
% scan_string(Index, CountsMap, FirstIndicesMap, StringList) -> {CountsMap, FirstIndicesMap}
scan_string(Index, Counts, Firsts, []) ->
    {Counts, Firsts};
scan_string(Index, Counts, Firsts, [Char|Rest]) ->
    if is_vowel(Char) ->
        % Update Counts
        NewCounts = maps:put(Char, maps:get(Char, Counts) + 1, Counts),
        
        % Update FirstIndices (only if not present)
        NewFirsts = case maps:get(Char, Firsts) of
                       undefined -> maps:put(Char, Index, Firsts);
                       _ -> Firsts
                   end,
        
        scan_string(Index + 1, NewCounts, NewFirsts, Rest)
    else
        % Consonants ignored for stats collection
        scan_string(Index + 1, Counts, Firsts, Rest)
    end.

%%% Helper function to compare two vowels based on frequency and first index
% compare_vowels(Vowel1, Vowel2, CountsMap, FirstIndicesMap) -> ComparisonResult
% Returns -1 if V1 comes before V2, 1 if V2 comes before V1, 0 if equal.
compare_vowels(V1, V2, Counts, Firsts) ->
    Freq1 = maps:get(V1, Counts),
    Freq2 = maps:get(V2, Counts),
    Idx1 = maps:get(V1, Firsts),
    Idx2 = maps:get(V2, Firsts),

    % Primary sort: Frequency DESC
    if Freq1 > Freq2 -> -1;
    Freq1 < Freq2 -> 1;
    % Secondary sort: First Index ASC
    Idx1 < Idx2 -> -1;
    Idx1 > Idx2 -> 1;
    true -> 0
    end.

%%% Main solver function
solve(S_list) ->
    % 1. Calculate statistics
    {Counts, Firsts} = scan_string(0, maps:new(), maps:new(), S_list),
    
    % Get unique vowels present
    VowelsPresent = maps:keys(Counts),

    % 2. Sort the unique vowels
    % We use a custom comparison function implemented via recursion or iteration over the list
    
    % Sorting implementation using standard list comparison approach
    sorted_vowels = lists:sort(
        fun(V1, V2) -> compare_vowels(V1, V2, Counts, Firsts) end, 
        VowelsPresent
    ),
    
    % 3. Build the new vowel sequence (flat list of chars)
    new_vowel_sequence = lists:flatten([
        lists:duplicate(maps:get(V, Counts), V) 
        || V <- sorted_vowels
    ]),

    % 4. Reconstruct the string
    
    % Use a recursive helper to iterate through S_list and new_vowel_sequence simultaneously
    reconstruct_string(S_list, new_vowel_sequence, [], 0, []),
    
% Helper for reconstruction
reconstruct_string([], _, Acc, _, _) -> lists:reverse(Acc).
reconstruct_string([Char|RestS], [], Acc, _, _) -> % Should not happen if counts match
    lists:reverse(Acc). 
reconstruct_string([Char|RestS], [NextVowel|RestV], Acc, SeqIdx, _) ->
    if is_vowel(Char) ->
        % Replace with next vowel from sorted sequence
        reconstruct_string(RestS, RestV, [NextVowel|Acc], SeqIdx + 1, 0)
    else
        % Consonant remains unchanged
        reconstruct_string(RestS, [NextVowel|RestV], [Char|Acc], SeqIdx, 0)
    end.

% Since the problem statement implies input/output strings, we wrap the function 
% to handle standard string/list conversion if necessary, assuming list of chars input/output.
% If the test environment uses binary strings, maps and lists need slight adjustments. 
% Assuming list of chars based on JS string interpretation.
```

    % We use a custom sort function based on comparing elements
    lists:sort(fun(T1, T2) ->
        {V1, C1, F1} = T1,
        {V2, C2, F2} = T2,

        % Primary comparison: Frequency (C2 - C1 for DESC)
        if C1 <> C2 ->
            C2 - C1
        % Secondary comparison: First Index (F1 - F2 for ASC)
        else
            F1 - F2
        end
    end, VowelData).


%% The main solving function
solve(String) ->

    {_, ReversedResult} = ReconstructionResult,

is_vowel(Char) when Char == 'a' -> true;
is_vowel(Char) when Char == 'e' -> true;
is_vowel(Char) when Char == 'i' -> true;
is_vowel(Char) when Char == 'o' -> true;
is_vowel(Char) when Char == 'u' -> true;
is_vowel(_) -> false.

% Helper to get the value associated with a key in a map, defaulting to 0 or handling absence.
get_map_val(Map, Key, Default) ->
    case maps:get(Key, Map) of
        {ok, V} -> V;
        error -> Default
    end.

solve(Str) ->
    % 1. Calculate frequency and track first appearance index
    % We use maps for storage: Map(Vowel, Count) and Map(Vowel, Index)
    {Frequencies, FirstIndices, VowelsPresent} = calculate_metadata(Str),

    % 2. Sort the unique vowels

    % 3. Build the new sequence of vowels (V_new)
    NewVowelSequence = build_vowel_sequence(SortedVowels, Frequencies),

    % 4. Reconstruct the string
    Result = reconstruct_string(Str, NewVowelSequence),
    
    % Convert list of chars/atoms to string representation (list of chars in Erlang context)
    Result.
    
%% calculate_metadata(Str) -> {Frequencies, FirstIndices, VowelsPresent}
calculate_metadata(Str) ->
    lists:foldl(
        fun(Acc, {I, Char}) ->
            case is_vowel(Char) of
                true ->
                    {Freqs, Indices, Vowels} = Acc,
                    
                    % Update Frequency
                    NewFreqs = maps:update(Char, fun(C) -> C + 1 end, Freqs),
                    
                    % Update First Index if not present
                    NewIndices = case maps:lookup(Char, Indices) of
                        {ok, _} -> Indices;
                        error -> maps:insert(Char, I, Indices)
                    end,
                    
                    % Update VowelsPresent set/list
                    NewVowels = case lists:member(Char, Vowels) of
                        true -> Vowels;
                        false -> [Char | Vowels]
                    end,
                    
                    {NewFreqs, NewIndices, NewVowels}
                false -> Acc
            end
        end,
        {maps:new(), maps:new(), []},
        lists:enumerate(Str)
    ).

%% sort_vowels(VowelsPresent, Frequencies, FirstIndices) -> SortedVowels
sort_vowels(VowelsPresent, Frequencies, FirstIndices) ->
    % Custom comparison function:
    % 1. Primary: Frequency (Descending)
    % 2. Secondary: First Index (Ascending)
    fun(V1, V2) ->
        Freq1 = get_map_val(Frequencies, V1, 0),
        Freq2 = get_map_val(Frequencies, V2, 0),
        
        case compare(Freq2, Freq1) of % Compare Freq2 to Freq1 for DESCENDING order
            order -> order;
            equal ->
                Index1 = get_map_val(FirstIndices, V1, 999999),
                Index2 = get_map_val(FirstIndices, V2, 999999),
                compare(Index1, Index2) % Ascending order for Index
        end
    end(VowelsPresent).

%% build_vowel_sequence(SortedVowels, Frequencies) -> [Vowel1, Vowel1, ..., Vowel2, ...]
build_vowel_sequence(SortedVowels, Frequencies) ->
    lists:foldl(
        fun(Acc, Vowel) ->
            Count = get_map_val(Frequencies, Vowel, 0),
            Repeat = lists:duplicate(Count, Vowel),
            [Repeat | Acc]
        end,
        [],
        SortedVowels
    ).

%% reconstruct_string(Str, NewVowelSequence) -> Result
reconstruct_string(Str, NewVowelSequence) ->
    % Helper function: reconstruct(OriginalString, NewSequence, CurrentIndexInSequence, ResultList)
    fun(Str, NewVowelSequence, 0, []) -> 
        [] % Base case: all vowels placed
    fun(Str, NewVowelSequence, SequenceIndex, Acc) ->
        case Str of
            [] -> Acc; % Should not happen if lengths match
            [C | T] ->
                case is_vowel(C) of
                    true ->
                        % It's a vowel, take the next element from the sequence
                        case NewVowelSequence of
                            [NextVowel | TSeq] ->
                                % Recurse, using the next element of the sequence and incrementing index
                                reconstruct_string(T, TSeq, SequenceIndex + 1, [NextVowel | Acc]);
                            _ -> 
                                % Safety break, sequence exhausted unexpectedly
                                [C | Acc]
                        end
                    false ->
                        % Consonant, keep it unchanged
                        reconstruct_string(T, NewVowelSequence, SequenceIndex, [C | Acc])
                end
        end
    end(Str, NewVowelSequence, 0, []) -> 
        % We need to reverse the accumulator because we prepended characters
        lists:reverse(reconstruct_string(Str, NewVowelSequence, 0, []))
    end(Str, NewVowelSequence, 0, []) ->
        % Initial call wrapper to ensure correct structure and termination
        reconstruct_string(Str, NewVowelSequence, 0, [])

% The actual implementation needs to handle the recursion properly to build the list in order.
% Let's redefine reconstruct_string recursively to build forward, which is easier.

reconstruct_string(Str, NewVowelSequence) ->
    fun(Str, NewVowelSequence, 0, []) -> 
        [] % Base case: original string exhausted
    fun(Str, NewVowelSequence, I, Acc) ->
        case Str of
            [] -> Acc;
            [C | TStr] ->
                if is_vowel(C) ->
                    case NewVowelSequence of
                        [NextVowel | TSeq] ->
                            % Vowel replacement
                            reconstruct_string(TStr, TSeq, I + 1, [NextVowel | Acc]);
                        _ -> 
                            % Should not happen if logic is sound
                            [C | Acc] 
                    end
                else
                    % Consonant remains
                    reconstruct_string(TStr, NewVowelSequence, I + 1, [C | Acc])
                end
        end
    end(Str, NewVowelSequence, 0, []).

% Final wrapper to meet the signature requirements
solve(Str) ->
    {Frequencies, FirstIndices, VowelsPresent} = calculate_metadata(Str),

    SortedVowels = sort_vowels(VowelsPresent, Frequencies, FirstIndices),

    NewVowelSequence = build_vowel_sequence(SortedVowels, Frequencies),

    Result = reconstruct_string(Str, NewVowelSequence),
    
    Result.
\
